claim: Meanwhile in a sample of more than 500 council wards where the BBC collected detailed voting figures, the party recorded an average vote share of 25% - not an especially high figure but still enough to put Reform well ahead of its rivals
article_title: Sir John Curtice: Election results show politics in the UK has fragmented

claim: In wards where more than 60% voted for Leave, support for Reform has averaged 40%
article_title: Sir John Curtice: Election results show politics in the UK has fragmented

claim: In contrast, in places where less than 40% backed Brexit, Reform won on average just 10%
article_title: Sir John Curtice: Election results show politics in the UK has fragmented
